User Story: As a returning user with iCloud data, I want Cubby to look for existing homes before starting a new inventory.
Expected Behavior: When onboarding is incomplete, there are no local homes, and CloudKit is enabled, the app shows "Looking for your homes" before allowing a new home setup after a delay.
Test Status: Passed focused UI test
Notes: CloudKit path is exercised with DEBUG-only FORCE_EXISTING_HOMES_RECOVERY to avoid iCloud account dependence; xcresult 2026.06.21_13-53-24 passed.

Feature: Set up new home after recovery wait
User Story: As a new user after CloudKit recovery, I want an explicit way to create a fresh home.
Expected Behavior: After the recovery delay, "Set Up a New Home" switches to the standard onboarding home-name form.
Test Status: Passed focused UI test
Notes: Verified 3-second recovery gate reveals Set Up a New Home and completes onboarding; xcresult 2026.06.21_13-53-24 passed.

User Story: As a first-time user, I want to name my first home so my inventory has a root container.
Expected Behavior: Entering a non-empty trimmed home name and tapping Get Started creates a home through AppStore, creates the default location in the repository, and marks onboarding complete.
Test Status: UI tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator for onboarding visibility and first-home form submission
Notes: Save errors are only logged with no visible onboarding error message; failure path still needs fault-injection validation.

User Story: As a returning user with existing local data, I want Cubby to skip onboarding and open my inventory.
Expected Behavior: If homes become available while onboarding is incomplete, Cubby chooses a preferred home, stores lastUsedHomeId, and marks onboarding complete.
Test Status: Unit tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator
Notes: Preferred-home selection is unit-covered; full LaunchContentView auto-complete UI path still needs direct validation.

User Story: As a user, I want a clear message if the runtime data stack cannot start.
Expected Behavior: If AppStore/Core Data setup fails, the app shows "Cubby Couldn't Start" with relaunch guidance.
Test Status: Passed focused UI test
Notes: Exercised fallback by launching with USE_CORE_DATA_SHARING_STACK=0; xcresult 2026.06.21_13-53-24 passed.

User Story: As a tester, I want deterministic seeded data for UI tests and snapshots.
Expected Behavior: UI-TESTING or -ui_testing uses in-memory storage, clears defaults, seeds data unless disabled, and marks onboarding complete unless SNAPSHOT_ONBOARDING is present.
Test Status: UI tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator across onboarding, seeded, empty, item-limit, missing-photo, shared, and hard-paywall launch states
Notes: Seed priority is encoded in CubbyApp.

User Story: As a developer, I want to preview seeded data outside UI tests.
Expected Behavior: SEED_MOCK_DATA and specialized seed arguments clear SwiftData seed data, create deterministic homes/locations/items, and set lastUsedHomeId.
Test Status: UI tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator using SEED_MOCK_DATA, SEED_ITEM_LIMIT_REACHED, and SEED_MISSING_LOCAL_PHOTO
Notes: Persistent non-UI-test seeding path still needs manual simulator validation.

User Story: As a user, I want production runtime persistence to use Core Data and CloudKit sharing.
Expected Behavior: When the sharing stack is enabled, the app creates a PersistenceController, migrates legacy SwiftData data when needed, wires AppStore to CoreDataAppRepository, and starts remote-change handling.
Test Status: Unit tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator
Notes: FeatureGate enables the Core Data sharing stack by default; full app startup with production CloudKit remains integration-level.

User Story: As a user sharing homes, I want owned and shared records stored in the correct CloudKit scopes.
Expected Behavior: The runtime creates Private.sqlite with private CloudKit scope and Shared.sqlite with shared CloudKit scope; repository objects preserve store placement for home/location/item graphs.
Test Status: Unit tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator
Notes: Store descriptions and repository placement are unit-covered; real CloudKit sync remains integration-level.

User Story: As an existing user, I want legacy SwiftData homes, locations, items, photos, emoji, and tags copied into Core Data.
Expected Behavior: Migration opens the legacy store when available, upserts homes, locations, parent relationships, and items into the private store, records completion, and retries later if the source is unavailable.
Test Status: Unit tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator
Notes: Core migration paths are unit-covered; live upgrade from an installed legacy app store remains integration-level.

User Story: As a user, I want Cubby to recover if migration fails.
Expected Behavior: On migration failure, Cubby resets Core Data stores, posts a recovery message, and AppStore refreshes while exposing recoveryMessage.
Test Status: Passed focused unit and UI tests
Defect Log: D013: recoveryMessage was held in AppStore but not surfaced to the user
Notes: Added Storage Recovered alert and DEBUG-only FORCE_MIGRATION_RECOVERY_MESSAGE hook; unit xcresult 2026.06.21_13-52-39 and UI xcresult 2026.06.21_13-55-17 passed.

User Story: As a user with iCloud changes, I want Cubby to refresh after remote store changes.
Expected Behavior: RemoteChangeHandler debounces NSPersistentStoreRemoteChange, processes pending changes, posts private/shared scope flags, and AppStore refreshes on the notification.
Test Status: Unit tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator
Notes: CloudSyncCoordinator also marks sync complete on remote-change notification; real CloudKit remote delivery still needs manual/integration validation.

User Story: As a user, I want unused local photo files cleaned up.
Expected Behavior: On startup, DataCleanupService reads active Core Data photoFileName values and deletes files in Documents/ItemPhotos that are no longer referenced.
Test Status: Unit test passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator preserving active photo metadata and deleting orphan file
Notes: Photo files are local only; startup invocation remains covered by code inspection.

User Story: As a developer or tester, I want explicit launch flags for CloudKit behavior.
Expected Behavior: UI tests and XCTest use in-memory non-CloudKit stores; DISABLE_CLOUDKIT uses a persistent local store; normal launches use CloudKit; strict startup and schema init flags are honored.
Test Status: Unit tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator
Notes: Forced availability launch args support account-state testing.

User Story: As a user, I want sync state to reflect iCloud availability.
Expected Behavior: CloudSyncState starts checking when enabled, disabled when off, maps available to synced, temporary/error to offline, and no-account/restricted/unknown to iCloud unavailable.
Test Status: Unit tests initially failed, then passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator
Defect Log: D001: CloudSyncCoordinator polling task could still perform its initial refresh after stop() if stop happened before the scheduled task executed; tests also used fixed sleeps that made the intended behavior race-prone.
Retest Status: Passed after focused and full unit retest
Notes: Need locate any visible sync UI before manual testing.

User Story: As a returning user, I want Cubby to reopen my last selected home.

Test Status: Unit tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator
Notes: Selection is also synchronized when homes change; UI-level launch behavior still needs simulator validation.

User Story: As a user with multiple homes, I want to switch between homes quickly.
Expected Behavior: The home title opens a popover listing homes; selecting one closes the popover, stores lastUsedHomeId, clears selectedLocation if it belongs to another home, and clears search via HomeView on selected home change.
Test Status: UI test passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator switching from Main Home to Beach House
Notes: Clearing selectedLocation/search on home change still needs direct assertion.

User Story: As a user, I want Pro, manage homes, and add-home actions near home selection.
Expected Behavior: The picker includes Cubby Pro, Manage Homes, and Add New Home rows; Pro opens status; Manage Homes enables edit mode; Add New Home checks free-tier gates before showing AddHomeView.
Test Status: UI tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator for Manage Homes entry/exit and Add New Home entry
Notes: Cubby Pro status action still needs direct UI validation.

User Story: As a user, I want to add another home when allowed.
Expected Behavior: AddHomeView trims the name, disables Save while blank, checks FeatureGate, creates the home through AppStore, selects the new home, and dismisses.
Test Status: UI test passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator adding Cabin while Pro
Notes: Repository-created Unsorted root location inferred by empty-state behavior; blank-name disabled state still needs direct assertion.

User Story: As a free user, I want clear upgrade guidance when I hit the home limit.

Test Status: Unit tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator

User Story: As a user, I want to delete an owned home only after confirmation.
Expected Behavior: In Manage Homes edit mode, deleting an owned home asks for confirmation, deletes the home and all items, refreshes selection, removes hidden shared-home state, and deletes associated local photos.
Test Status: UI confirmation test and unit cleanup tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator
Notes: Collaborator shared homes use leave instead of delete; destructive confirm path still needs end-to-end UI deletion validation.

User Story: As a collaborator, I want to leave a shared home without deleting the owner's data.

Test Status: UI confirmation and unit behavior tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator
Notes: Owners cannot leave their own share; confirming the destructive leave action end-to-end still needs direct UI validation.

User Story: As a user, I want home items grouped by storage location path.
Expected Behavior: HomeView groups selected-home items by storageLocationID, hides empty locations, sorts sections by full path, and sorts items alphabetically inside each section.
Test Status: UI snapshot and section-collapse tests passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator for visible grouped seeded items
Notes: Empty locations remain available in location management and pickers; sort order needs direct assertion if critical.

User Story: As a user with many items, I want to collapse location sections.

Test Status: UI test passed 2026-06-21 on iPhone 16e simulator
Notes: Accessibility action and search-disabled collapse behavior still need direct validation.
